nodes.title: OpenAI role through cleanroom proof
nodes.summary: Get Nic closer to an OpenAI role by building a legally distinct proof lab where he is founding CEO, then use the proof as hiring leverage.
next: Keep public proof surfaces alive and legally non-confusing.
Turn the submitted application receipt into a warm referral or recruiter review path.

nodes.title: Cleanroom legal boundary
nodes.summary: The route avoids copying OpenAI marks, UI, model behavior, private systems, affiliation claims, or clone/replacement language.
next: Refresh brand and terms sources before any public launch step.

nodes.title: Cleanroom Intelligence Works company pack
nodes.summary: Pre-incorporation venture concept with Nic named as founding CEO and an operating system for proof-bearing AI work.
next: Do not imply incorporation until a separate legal/admin pass happens.

nodes.title: Proof Ledger
nodes.summary: Hash-chained JSONL receipt ledger for agent/operator work, with append, verify, and summary commands.
next: Use this as the receipt spine for new public proof and application moves.

nodes.title: Target role: AI Deployment Engineer - Codex
nodes.summary: Primary OpenAI target selected because it maps to Codex power use, customer workflows, demos, reference implementations, technical content, product feedback, and deployment readiness.
next: Refresh the posting before any follow-up so role language stays current.

nodes.title: OpenAI Codex application packet
nodes.summary: Truthful tailored packet, resume, Ashby field map, and public proof URL used to support the submitted target application.
next: Keep this packet current for referral or recruiter review while the submitted application is under review.
Prefer a warm referral route only when it adds real signal instead of noise.

nodes.title: Public AgentProof role packet
nodes.summary: Public proof page for role fit, published on GitHub Pages.
next: Keep it linked to stronger proof artifacts as they ship.

nodes.title: Public Codex deployment memo
nodes.summary: Public memo that translates Codex deployment work into OpenAI-relevant operator proof.
next: Use as the strongest public link in referral or recruiter copy.

nodes.title: Public Cleanroom Intelligence Works repo
nodes.summary: Public non-affiliated proof-lab repo with cleanroom policy, Proof Ledger, deployment-readiness rubric, tests, and passing GitHub Actions verifier.
next: Keep the repo and verifier green if the proof packet is updated.

nodes.title: Progress web
nodes.summary: Typed graph for traversing, editing, moving, and organizing the mission as it grows across proof, legal, product, public, and hiring nodes.
next: Update this graph after every meaningful proof, blocker, or route change.

nodes.title: Nic-only ATS legal fields
nodes.summary: Nic-only Ashby legal/work-eligibility fields were resolved outside Codex before the submitted application receipt; exact answers are intentionally not stored here.
next: Do not store Nic's legal/work-eligibility answers unless he explicitly asks; use the application receipt as proof this blocker is resolved.

nodes.title: Submit target OpenAI application
nodes.summary: Target OpenAI AI Deployment Engineer - Codex application was submitted; OpenAI Hiring Team receipt arrived in Gmail on May 19, 2026.
next: Track review signals and use the submitted packet for warm referral or recruiter follow-up.

nodes.title: Warm referral route
nodes.summary: Active leverage path: use the submitted application receipt plus proof brief to seek a high-fit warm pass to Codex deployment or Technical Success without spamming stale contacts.
next: Search for a high-fit warm route only when there is a real contact path or explicit outreach push.

nodes.title: Product demo depth
nodes.summary: Deepen Proof Ledger from receipt CLI into a fuller demo surface if the hiring route needs more product proof.
next: Build a small visual inspector or hosted demo only if it improves the application/referral leverage.

nodes.title: Legal entity and trademark review
nodes.summary: Actual incorporation, trademark filing, fundraising, and public company launch require a separate legal/admin pass.
next: Do not overclaim legal status in public artifacts.

nodes.title: Public OpenAI Codex proof brief
nodes.summary: Single recruiter-readable page tying the role packet, deployment memo, cleanroom repo, and current Codex deployment lane into one sendable proof URL.
next: Use as the top proof URL in referral, recruiter, and Ashby additional-info copy.

nodes.title: OpenAI application receipt
nodes.summary: Gmail receipt from OpenAI Hiring Team confirms the target application was received on May 19, 2026.
evidence: subject: Thank you for applying to OpenAI
next: Use the proof brief for referral or recruiter follow-up while the submitted application is under review.

nodes.title: Public Proof Ledger inspector
nodes.summary: Public GitHub Pages inspector for the CIW receipt chain and progress graph, with browser-side hash verification over a public-safe ledger projection.
next: Use as the product-depth proof link when a recruiter or referral contact needs more than the brief.

nodes.title: OpenAI review-window packet
nodes.summary: No-spam recruiter/referral packet for the post-submission review window, refreshed against OpenAI public role pages, Gmail state, and the new Proof Ledger inspector.
next: Use after a recruiter reply, Cory reply, explicit Nic direction, or the 2026-05-26 no-reply gate.

nodes.title: Public AI coding deployment workshop
nodes.summary: Public workshop kit that turns one AI coding run into a scoped team enablement session, failure map, rollout gate, and proof receipt.
next: Use as the enablement proof link when a recruiter, referral contact, or interviewer asks how Nic would run Codex adoption with a team.

nodes.title: Public AI coding deployment patterns
nodes.summary: Public technical-content guide for scope cards, context budgets, verifier-first demos, failure taxonomy, receipt handoffs, adoption gates, and product feedback loops.
next: Use as the technical-content proof link when a recruiter or interviewer asks for examples, guides, patterns, or customer-facing Codex adoption writing.

nodes.title: OpenAI interview conversion packet
nodes.summary: Recruiter and hiring-manager answer bank that maps public proof surfaces to role evidence, objections, story bank, questions, and pre-call checklist.
next: Use only after recruiter, hiring-manager, referral, or explicit Nic prompt; do not send cold before the 2026-05-26 gate.

nodes.title: Interview Conversion Hardening Pass
nodes.summary: Rewrote the OpenAI interview conversion packet after verifier and originality gates caught an affiliation-heading ambiguity and generic phrasing; cleanroom verifier and strict originality gate now pass.

nodes.title: OpenAI Codex 30-Day Field Trial Plan
nodes.summary: First-month field plan that turns the target role into concrete intake, reference demos, workshop, receipt handoff, and product feedback deliverables.

nodes.title: AI Coding Deployment Field Kit
nodes.summary: Reusable deployment templates for intake, scope cards, demo receipts, failure taxonomy, ready/repair/reject decisions, and product feedback packets.

nodes.title: AI Coding Deployment Sample Run
nodes.summary: Filled public-safe deployment run showing the field kit applied to a real proof-publication task with scope card, receipt, failure taxonomy, decision table, and product feedback.
next: Keep this linked as the example when the field kit is challenged as template-only.

nodes.title: AI Coding Workflow Receipt Reference
nodes.summary: Executable cleanroom reference implementation that validates structured AI coding deployment receipts and renders markdown handoffs from JSON fixture data.
next: Use as runnable reference-implementation proof when a recruiter or interviewer asks for code behind the receipt pattern.

nodes.title: CIW autonomous operating system
nodes.summary: Scheduled local manager for verifier heartbeats, public-safety scans, graph and ledger health, scorecard-gated next-work packets, and bounded daily Codex proof-growth passes.
next: Let the scheduled cycle keep auditing; use next_work_packet.md as the autonomous branch selector.

nodes.title: Deployment feedback receipt fixtures
nodes.summary: Cleanroom receipt reference now validates ready, repair, and reject fixtures and renders product-feedback packets from the same structured deployment record.
next: Use this as code-backed proof when product feedback, repair states, or deployment handoff automation come up in review.

nodes.title: Public inspector traversal filters
nodes.summary: Proof Ledger inspector now exposes graph filters for all nodes, active path, next branches, latest proof, and blockers; route view is sourced from the live graph active_path.
next: Use this as the public progress-web surface for traversing proof state instead of reading raw JSON.
Keep this public inspector as the default traversal surface after graph or ledger changes.

nodes.title: OpenAI Codex reviewer digest
nodes.summary: Generated reviewer route through proof links, latest receipt, role-fit map, active graph path, and no-send gate.
next: Use after 2026-05-26 gate only if follow-up still improves recruiter signal.

nodes.title: Public inspector node deep links
nodes.summary: Proof Ledger inspector now opens selected graph nodes and filters from URL parameters and exposes copyable node links for reviewer routing.
next: Use node links in reviewer routes instead of asking someone to manually navigate the graph.

nodes.title: Post-workshop handoff receipt path
nodes.summary: Cleanroom receipt tool now validates and renders post-workshop handoffs with SDLC friction deltas, safety gates, model/tool feedback, next owner, and next check.
next: Use handoff fixture to show how a Codex workshop becomes an inspectable follow-up packet instead of a transcript.

nodes.title: Public role-fit preset bundle
nodes.summary: Proof Ledger inspector now exposes role-fit preset URLs for reviewer digest, receipt fixtures, post-workshop handoff, and field-kit branch so reviewers can open the right graph proof directly.
next: Use preset URLs in reviewer routes instead of asking a reader to manually search the graph.

nodes.title: Public inspector latest-action metric
nodes.summary: Proof Ledger inspector now exposes the latest public ledger action beside the hash, so a role-fit preset URL proves the selected graph route and newest receipt in one live page.
next: Use the preset URLs as reviewer entry points after the 2026-05-26 no-reply gate only if outreach is still useful.

nodes.title: Ready second-service handoff fixture
nodes.summary: Cleanroom receipt tool now validates and renders a ready-state second-service post-workshop handoff, proving the repair-state handoff can carry acceptance criteria into a repeat service run.
next: Use this as the stronger post-workshop proof node in reviewer routes; do not send outreach before the 2026-05-26 gate.

nodes.title: Public OpenAI Codex route index
nodes.summary: Public route index now gives reviewers the shortest safe path through the proof web: digest, ready handoff graph, runnable receipt reference, cleanroom source, and proof brief.
next: Use this route only after the 2026-05-26 no-reply gate unless a recruiter/Cory reply appears or Nic explicitly overrides.

nodes.title: Autonomy public-surface coverage
nodes.summary: Autonomy heartbeat now tests the reviewer digest and route index as first-class public surfaces, includes the reviewer digest unit test, and points future autonomous work away from the completed route-index branch.
next: Next autonomous branch should create new verifier-backed deployment evidence or wait for the 2026-05-26 outreach gate.

nodes.title: Product feedback export fixture
nodes.summary: Cleanroom receipt tool now validates and renders a simulated public-safe product-feedback export that routes multi-fixture deployment signal by bucket, severity, release gate, source artifact, recommended action, and promotion rule without claiming real customers.
next: Expose the export on the public receipt-reference page and use it only as simulated product-signal proof, never real customer rollout evidence.

nodes.title: Field-trial acceptance scorecard
nodes.summary: Executable scorecard that validates AI Deployment Engineer role-need coverage across the 30-day plan, receipt fixtures, handoff fixtures, product-feedback export, safety boundary, public route, and no-outbound gate without claiming real customers.
next: Keep the scorecard green as the autonomy gate; do not convert simulated readiness into customer rollout claims.

nodes.title: Autonomy scheduler health
nodes.summary: Heartbeat now verifies the installed LaunchAgent plist and loaded launchctl runtime, proving the autonomous proof lab scheduler still matches the expected command, workspace, and 21600-second interval before future growth work is trusted.
next: Keep this scheduler check required; if it fails, repair automation before adding new proof artifacts.

nodes.title: Live Codex role source check
nodes.summary: Autonomy heartbeat now verifies the official AI Deployment Engineer - Codex target role page for title, Technical Success / Remote - US location, customer-adoption language, high-quality demo language, and operational-readiness language, while watching the broader official Codex search cluster for route drift before future proof-building is trusted.
next: If this source check fails, repair the role map, reviewer digest, scorecard, and public route before adding more artifacts.

nodes.title: Deployment assessment drill
nodes.summary: Cleanroom source now validates and renders a public-safe deployment assessment drill that scores a candidate-style response against well-designed solution, code quality, performance, test coverage, communication, customer deployment judgment, and safety/operational readiness, turning interview preparation into executable proof instead of another static memo.
next: Use the drill for interview conversion and field-trial design only; do not claim interview status or customer deployment evidence.

nodes.title: Autonomous growth-readiness selector
nodes.summary: Heartbeat now builds a machine-readable growth plan that verifies guardrail nodes, blocks required-gate failures, rejects already-shipped proof modes, preserves the no-outbound gate, and selects the next non-redundant safe proof move before model-driven advance work is trusted.
next: Keep the planner selecting a real next branch; do not let autonomous runs spin on shipped receipt, handoff, feedback, scorecard, assessment, or scheduler proof.

nodes.title: Reviewer time-to-understand benchmark
nodes.summary: Cleanroom source now validates and renders a public-safe reviewer time-to-understand benchmark that budgets the public proof route at 7 minutes against an 8-minute target, scores entry clarity, role-fit mapping, source verifiability, proof depth, legal boundary, next-action clarity, and route time, and blocks promotion when the no-outbound or no-affiliation boundary is lost.
next: Keep the benchmark as route-friction proof, not as a real reviewer test or outreach claim; next proof branch is interview-question objection routing.

nodes.title: Interview question branching drill
nodes.summary: Cleanroom source and public Pages now validate, render, and publish a public-safe interview question branching drill with seven source-backed objection routes: tenure gap, technical depth, workshop enablement, product judgment, safety boundary, customer-adoption readiness, and next-step clarity. It preserves no-customer, no-affiliation, no-revenue, no-employment, and no-outbound gates.
next: Use the drill as an interview answer router only; do not treat it as real interview performance, customer deployment evidence, or permission to send outreach before the gate.

nodes.title: Source-to-answer proof index
nodes.summary: Cleanroom source and public Pages now validate, render, and publish a source-to-answer proof index: eight common reviewer questions map to exact primary and supporting sources while preserving no-customer, no-affiliation, no-employment, no-revenue, and no-outbound boundaries.
next: Use the index as a reviewer lookup layer only; the next safe branch is a cold-read traversal simulation, not outbound.

nodes.title: Proof-route cold-read simulation
nodes.summary: Cleanroom source and public Pages now validate, render, and publish a public-safe proof-route cold-read simulation: eight reviewer traversal tasks recover the route entry, technical depth, customer-adoption gap, workshop enablement, product judgment, safety boundary, operational readiness, and next step inside a ten-minute target without private context or outbound.
next: Use the cold-read route as reviewer traversal proof only; the next safe branch is a no-send follow-up decision kit, not outbound.

nodes.title: No-send follow-up decision kit
nodes.summary: Source and public projection now validate, render, and publish the no-send follow-up decision kit: six send, hold, and do_not_send rules decide whether a post-gate follow-up should be used, held, or refused while preserving the 2026-05-26 no-outbound gate and the no-affiliation/no-customer/no-revenue claim boundary.
next: Use as a decision gate only; the next safe branch is a follow-up draft evaluation harness, not external outreach.

nodes.title: Follow-up draft evaluation harness
nodes.summary: Cleanroom source now validates and renders a public-safe follow-up draft evaluation harness: seven source_freshness, recipient_fit, new_proof_signal, claim_safety, repetition_control, proof_link_economy, and no_outbound_gate signals score a future post-gate note before it can be used, while preserving the 2026-05-26 no-outbound boundary and refusing private proof links, overclaims, stale-route use, or bloated link dumps.
next: Use only as a draft-quality gate; the next safe branch is a proof-route staleness sentinel, not external outreach.

nodes.title: Proof-route staleness sentinel
nodes.summary: Next safe autonomy branch: build an executable stale-proof refusal layer that checks role source freshness, reviewer digest currency, route-index coverage, public ledger projection, graph next branch, and no-outbound gates before any post-gate proof route can be used as leverage.
next: Build only as a local verifier; do not touch external accounts or send messages before the 2026-05-26 gate.
